properties.name: Teotihuacan
properties.description: One of the largest cities of the ancient world at its peak, home to the Pyramid of the Sun and Pyramid of the Moon. Its influence extended across Mesoamerica.

properties.name: Tenochtitlan
properties.description: Capital of the Aztec Triple Alliance, built on an island in Lake Texcoco. Population estimates range from 200,000 to 400,000 at its height.

properties.name: Cholula
properties.description: Home to the Great Pyramid of Cholula, the largest pyramid by volume in the world. Occupied continuously by multiple cultures over nearly two millennia.

properties.name: Tula (Tollán)
properties.description: Capital of the Toltec state, known for the famous Atlantean warrior columns. Toltec cultural influence spread widely through trade and migration.

properties.name: Xochicalco
properties.description: Fortified hilltop city that rose to prominence after Teotihuacan's fall. Important astronomical observatory and calendar reform site.
properties.significance: Post-classic hilltop fortress and trade hub

properties.name: Monte Albán
properties.description: Capital of the Zapotec civilization, built atop a mountain overlooking the Valley of Oaxaca. Features elaborate tombs, ball courts, and an astronomical observatory.

properties.name: Mitla
properties.description: Known for its extraordinary geometric mosaic stonework. Served as a major religious center and was the most important Zapotec site after Monte Albán's decline.

properties.name: El Tajín
properties.description: Famous for the Pyramid of the Niches with its 365 niches — one for each day of the solar year. Center of the Classic Veracruz culture.

properties.name: San Lorenzo
properties.description: The earliest known major Olmec center, home to colossal stone heads. San Lorenzo was the dominant center of early Olmec civilization.

properties.name: La Venta
properties.description: Succeeded San Lorenzo as the dominant Olmec capital. Known for its massive earthen pyramid, complex of mosaics, and offerings including jade figurines.

properties.name: Tres Zapotes
properties.description: Third major Olmec center, occupied for over a millennium. Home to Stela C, which bears one of the earliest Long Count calendar dates yet discovered.

properties.name: Chichen Itza
properties.description: One of the largest Maya cities, known for El Castillo (the Temple of Kukulcan), the Great Ball Court, and the Sacred Cenote. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.
properties.significance: Dominant Maya city of the Terminal Classic

properties.name: Uxmal
properties.description: Capital of the Puuc Maya region, renowned for its intricate façade decoration. The Pyramid of the Magician is its most iconic structure.

properties.name: Dzibilchaltún
properties.description: One of the oldest and longest continuously occupied Maya sites. The Temple of the Seven Dolls is aligned to the rising sun on the equinoxes.

properties.name: Tikal
properties.description: One of the most powerful Maya cities of the Classic period, with temples rising above the jungle canopy. Its dynastic wars with Calakmul shaped Classic Maya politics.

properties.name: El Mirador
properties.description: Home to the La Danta complex, possibly the largest pyramid by volume in the ancient world. El Mirador was the dominant Preclassic Maya city.

properties.name: Copán
properties.description: Known as the 'Athens of the Maya world' for its extraordinary sculpture, hieroglyphic stairway, and stelae. A center of art, astronomy, and calendrics.
properties.significance: Maya center of art and hieroglyphic writing

properties.name: Kaminaljuyú
properties.description: Major highland Maya city that controlled obsidian and cacao trade routes. Showed strong Teotihuacan influence during the Early Classic period.
properties.significance: Highland Maya trade and ceremonial hub

properties.name: Takalik Abaj
properties.description: Unique site showing the transition from Olmec to Maya cultural traditions. Contains both Olmec-style monuments and early Maya hieroglyphic inscriptions.

properties.name: Palenque
properties.description: Known for its refined art and architecture, including the Temple of the Inscriptions — the tomb of the ruler K'inich Janaab' Pakal. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.
properties.significance: Major Classic Maya kingdom and art center

properties.name: Quiriguá
properties.description: Famous for its monumental stelae and zoomorphs — some of the tallest stone monuments in the ancient Maya world. Broke free from Copán's dominance in 738 CE.
